출애굽기 7:1.content: Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, I have made you as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet.

출애굽기 7:2.content: You shall speak all that I command you; and Aaron your brother shall speak to Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.

출애굽기 7:3.content: I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.

출애굽기 7:4.content: But Pharaoh will not listen to you, so I will lay my hand on Egypt, and bring out my armies, my people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.

출애굽기 7:5.content: The Egyptians shall know that I am Yahweh when I stretch out my hand on Egypt, and bring the children of Israel out from among them.”

출애굽기 7:6.content: Moses and Aaron did so. As Yahweh commanded them, so they did.

출애굽기 7:7.content: Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three years old, when they spoke to Pharaoh.

출애굽기 7:8.content: Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

출애굽기 7:9.content: “When Pharaoh speaks to you, saying, ‘Perform a miracle!’ then you shall tell Aaron, ‘Take your rod, and cast it down before Pharaoh, and it will become a serpent.’ ”

출애굽기 7:10.content: Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and they did so, as Yahweh had commanded. Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh and before his servants, and it became a serpent.

출애굽기 7:11.content: Then Pharaoh also called for the wise men and the sorcerers. They also, the magicians of Egypt, did the same thing with their enchantments.

출애굽기 7:12.content: For they each cast down their rods, and they became serpents; but Aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.

출애굽기 7:13.content: Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he didn’t listen to them, as Yahweh had spoken.

출애굽기 7:14.content: Yahweh said to Moses, “Pharaoh’s heart is stubborn. He refuses to let the people go.

출애굽기 7:15.content: Go to Pharaoh in the morning. Behold, he is going out to the water. You shall stand by the river’s bank to meet him. You shall take the rod which was turned to a serpent in your hand.

출애굽기 7:16.content: You shall tell him, ‘Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, has sent me to you, saying, “Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness. Behold, until now you haven’t listened.”

출애굽기 7:17.content: Yahweh says, “In this you shall know that I am Yahweh. Behold: I will strike with the rod that is in my hand on the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.

출애굽기 7:18.content: The fish that are in the river will die and the river will become foul. The Egyptians will loathe to drink water from the river.” ’ ”

출애굽기 7:19.content: Yahweh said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Take your rod, and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, over their streams, and over their pools, and over all their ponds of water, that they may become blood. There will be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.’ ”

출애굽기 7:20.content: Moses and Aaron did so, as Yahweh commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and struck the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.

출애굽기 7:21.content: The fish that were in the river died. The river became foul. The Egyptians couldn’t drink water from the river. The blood was throughout all the land of Egypt.

출애굽기 7:22.content: The magicians of Egypt did the same thing with their enchantments. So Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he didn’t listen to them, as Yahweh had spoken.

출애굽기 7:23.content: Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he didn’t even take this to heart.

출애굽기 7:24.content: All the Egyptians dug around the river for water to drink; for they couldn’t drink the river water.

출애굽기 7:25.content: Seven days were fulfilled, after Yahweh had struck the river.

출애굽기 7:26.content: Yahweh spoke to Moses, “Go in to Pharaoh, and tell him, ‘This is what Yahweh says, “Let my people go, that they may serve me.

출애굽기 7:27.content: If you refuse to let them go, behold, I will plague all your borders with frogs.

출애굽기 7:28.content: The river will swarm with frogs, which will go up and come into your house, and into your bedroom, and on your bed, and into the house of your servants, and on your people, and into your ovens, and into your kneading troughs.

출애굽기 7:29.content: The frogs shall come up both on you, and on your people, and on all your servants.” ’ ”
